title: From symplectic geometry to combinatorics and back
speaker: Dan Cristofaro-Gardiner
abstract: A much studied combinatorial object associated to a polytope is its "Ehrhart function". This is typically studied for integral or rational polytopes. The first part of the talk will be about joint work with Li and Stanley extending this theory to certain irrational polytopes; the idea for this was inspired by work of McDuff and Schlenk on symplectic embeddings of four-dimensional ellipsoids.

proof_stages.label: Classical Ehrhart framework and irrational gap
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Ehrhart counting functions, recalls the polynomial/quasi-polynomial and reciprocity theorems for integral and rational polytopes, and then isolates the irrational-vertex case as essentially unstudied.

proof_stages.label: Ellipsoid viewpoint on irrational triangles
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains that special irrational right triangles naturally appear to symplectic geometers as ellipsoids, suggesting that irrational Ehrhart theory should be sought under geometric restrictions.

proof_stages.label: McDuff-Schlenk Fibonacci staircase
proof_stages.summary: The ellipsoid-into-ball embedding function c(a) is reviewed, including the volume bound, the Fibonacci staircase, and its irrational golden-mean accumulation point.

proof_stages.label: ECH capacities as lattice-counting obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Embedded contact homology capacities are introduced as monotone obstructions to four-dimensional symplectic embeddings, their ellipsoid values are expressed by the ordered sequences N(a,b), and entries of these sequences are reinterpreted as lattice points in right triangles.

proof_stages.label: Fibonacci-staircase lattice inequalities
proof_stages.summary: Combining McDuff-Schlenk embeddings with ECH capacities yields non-obvious inequalities between lattice counts, equivalently Ehrhart functions, for two Fibonacci-defined families of triangles, motivating a direct combinatorial explanation.

proof_stages.label: Ehrhart equality and golden-mean polynomial limit
proof_stages.summary: Kleinman and the speaker strengthen the inequality to equality of Ehrhart polynomials for the finite Fibonacci triangles, and passing to the limit gives an irrational golden-mean triangle whose Ehrhart function is polynomial.

proof_stages.label: Primitive quasi-polynomial irrational triangles
proof_stages.summary: For a right triangle T_{u,v} with irrational slope ratio, the work with Li and Stanley characterizes primitive quasi-polynomial Ehrhart behavior by the integer conditions u+v=alpha and 1/u+1/v=beta, forcing special quadratic irrational vertices and preserving Ehrhart-type properties.

proof_stages.label: Return to symplectic embeddings and dynamics
proof_stages.summary: The irrational Ehrhart criteria are then used as a guide for ellipsoid embeddings into closed symplectic toric manifolds, predicting twelve infinite-staircase cases, and are suggested as useful for ECH dynamical orbit-count questions.
